response.justification: Sarah Smith is a seasoned full-stack engineer with 15+ years of broad software development experience and genuine depth in TypeScript, Node.js, and distributed systems. However, she falls significantly short of the role's core requirement of 8+ years building production-grade AI solutions — her AI-specific work is limited to roughly 13 months at re:cinq, where she contributes to MCP tooling and agentic workflows. Python, a primary language for this role, does not appear anywhere in her skills or employment history. There is no evidence of vector database usage, RAG architecture design, LLM evaluation pipelines, or deploying AI systems on cloud platforms, all of which are explicit requirements for a Staff-level GenAI engineer.
strengths: Hands-on MCP (Model Context Protocol) server and agentic workflow experience at re:cinq directly maps to a listed key technology
Production-scale distributed systems background (Dunnhumby: millions of HTTP req/s) demonstrates ability to build high-throughput data infrastructure
Consistent staff-level seniority across multiple employers, with evidence of mentoring, open-source leadership, and public knowledge sharing
concerns: AI experience is effectively limited to ~13 months; the role requires 8+ years of production-grade AI solution delivery — a gap that cannot be bridged by adjacent engineering experience
Python is entirely absent from her listed skills, employment history, and open-source projects despite being the primary language called out in the job description
No evidence of vector databases, RAG architectures, LLM performance optimization, multi-agent frameworks, or cloud-based AI deployments (AWS/GCP/Azure AI services)
